seed: restlessness vs peace; memory_vs_motion; diagonal_argument; edge_crawl; flame against shatter; closed_corners; light_vs_dark; patch; signal_noise | abstract variation request 197; target batch count 500
title: Clash of Calm
reasoning: This artwork explores the tension between restlessness and peace through contrasting colors and patterns. The chosen palette of lime, black, electric blue, and magenta reflects the emotional spectrum: lime offers a breath of peace, while black anchors the restless energy. The arrangement splits vertically; the left side embodies static calm with seamless fields of lime and black, while the right disrupts with a dynamic pattern of electric blue and magenta, embodying motion and conflict. The central collision occurs at the border, where the heavy dark mass of black confronts the vibrant accents, creating a vivid visual argument. The edge style features hard edges, emphasizing the abruptness of these emotional states. Broken pixels introduce intentional irregularities, suggesting the struggle between these conflicting feelings, ultimately cultivating a rich narrative of tension and release.

layout.name: split_vertical
layout.description: Left 4 columns are emotion A, right 4 columns are emotion B.

composition.name: light_vs_weight
composition.idea: A small bright shape fights against a heavy dark mass.

referenceExample.title: Dread Releasing
referenceExample.pattern: heavy top corner loosens into pale open space

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
